Precision oncology of SPAtial immune esCape mEchanisms in ovarian cancerSPACE bi-weekly meeting
1Ziqi Kang Mar 28th, 2025

Meeting Agenda•Updates on the spatial analysis
•The scale of cell-cell interaction
•Immune cells aggregate on TSI contributes more for tumor prognosis? 
•Revisit: Cell interactions we want to focus?
•Updates about the deconvolution analysis. -> Already meet with Daria
•iMTB reports -> meet with Angela

Tissue landscape patterns Myelonet + Lymphonet Immune hotspot

To describe tissue landscape: 1. Cell-cell interaction scale 2. Global immune-TSI distance 3. Immune from stroma/tumor side 4. Network structure analysis

Cell-cell distance landscape
•All values in the following pages are transferred to µm.

Cell-cell distance landscape

Calculate Tumor-Stromal interface with new value•Use max_dist = 26, the detected TSI improved!

Calculate Tumor-Stromal interface with new value•Use max_dist = 26
•The KDTree-detected TSI seems to be less continuity than RCN-detected TSI. But the KDTree-TSI better preserved some shapes. 
•In the following TSI-related analysis I still use the RCN-TSI.

Global Immune-TSI study•Here calculating the distance of each immune cell type to TSI, use the median value. Use “Mann-Whitney" test. 
•CD4T, CD8T and NK cells have longer immune-TSI distance in HRP than HRD. •CD4T and NK cells have longer immune-TSI distance in patients who have progression. 
•One thing to improve: also calculate the median distance of global immune cell type to TSI (?)

Deﬁne tumor/stroma-resident immune cells•Use radius=80µm by checking the peak of distance between cancer and immune cells. 
•For each immune cell, we identiﬁed all neighboring cells within a certain radius. 
•If >50% of neighboring cells were annotated as tumor cells, the immune cell was considered tumor-resident; otherwise stroma-resident.

Deﬁne immune exclusion index (IEI)•Calculate immune density in each region by immune cell count / the total number of tumor/stroma cells. 
•Deﬁne immune exclusion index (IEI) as immune_density_stroma / immune_density_tumor (is this correct?)
High immune exclusion: S039, IEI=30 Low immune exclusion: S201, IEI=0.3 Mid immune exclusion: S119, IEI=4.2

Deﬁne immune exclusion index (IEI)•Calculate immune density in each region by immune cell count / the total number of tumor/stroma cells. 
•Deﬁne immune exclusion index (IEI) as immune_density_stroma / immune_density_tumor (is this correct?)
High immune exclusion: S039, IEI=30 Low immune exclusion: S201, IEI=0.3 Mid immune exclusion: S119, IEI=4.2
Question: IEI should be normalised by tumor/stroma ratio? - check if correlations? Might need to remove the coeﬃcient - eﬀect are diﬀerent among lymphoid or myeloid

Deﬁne immune exclusion index (IEI)•Calculate immune density in each region by immune cell count / the total number of tumor/stroma cells. 
•Deﬁne immune exclusion index (IEI) as immune_density_stroma / immune_density_tumor (is this correct?)
High immune exclusion: S039, IEI=30 Low immune exclusion: S201, IEI=0.3 Mid immune exclusion: S119, IEI=4.2

Deﬁne immune exclusion index (IEI)•BRCAwt has higher CD4T density in both stroma and tumor regions. 
•Patient with progression has higher NK density in stroma regions. 
CD4TNK

Deﬁne immune exclusion index (IEI)•HRP patients have higher IEI of B cells - B cells tend to be enriched in stroma region. •BRCAwt patients have lower IEI of macrophages - macrophages tend to be enriched in tumor region. B
Macrophage

Immune - TSI distance from tumor or stromal side? (Median distance)•Calculated for global immune cells •HRP groups, immune cells are further to TSI in stromal side.

Immune - TSI distance from tumor or stromal side? (Median distance)•Tumor side (left) and stromal side (right), calculated for each cell type •Immune cell aggreate on TSI -> good prognosis?

Immune - TSI distance from tumor or stromal side? (Peak value)•What is the distance that the majority of immune cells located in? 
•The peak values of the stromal side form 3 clusters?

Network analysis: choosing threshold for longest edge•The idea is to use the elbow-shaped curve. 
•Need to explore more how people study immune networks - the connectivity and the size of the network, etc. 
•P .S. This is an old image thus the unit is µm here ->

Network analysis: Previous results•Previous results found the largest Lymphonet component size has diﬀerence across HRP/HRD groups. -> TLS.

Network analysis: Previous results•Previous results found the largest Lymphonet component size has diﬀerence across HRP/HRD groups. -> TLS. 
S027: 25030, S109: 6070S102: 2986S149: 420

Network analysis: CAF network•Adding CAF network in the analysis, we could ﬁnd the HRP-CAF network have larger edge length. -> Sparse CAF network? 
•One thought is current study on network is not enough - do not only focus on largest components, etc

Plan for next step, other questions•How to improve the network analysis? 
•The relationship of immune cells and TSI? 
•Immune inﬁltration model (on my notebook)
•Building blocks writing with Obsidian
•Xiao Fu’s visit on April
•My thesis committee meeting in May 5th
•Computational restrictions - High utilization with the old Linux workstation

Let’s book the next meeting slotThanks for listening!
